entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: Arachis hypogaea L., a crop pivotal to diets and economies in semi-arid regions

entities.name: GENETIC PURITY
entities.description: The state of a crop variety being genetically uniform and true to type

entities.name: SEED CERTIFICATION
entities.description: The process of ensuring that seeds meet specific genetic and quality standards

entities.name: CLIMATE CHANGE
entities.description: Global environmental shifts causing food security challenges

entities.name: SUSTAINABLE AGRICULTURE
entities.description: Agricultural practices that meet current needs without compromising future generations

entities.name: SEMI-ARID REGIONS
entities.description: Areas characterized by low rainfall where groundnut is economically important

entities.name: PHENOTYPIC SEED CERTIFICATION
entities.description: Traditional methods of seed certification based on morphological characteristics

entities.name: MORPHOLOGICAL SIMILARITIES
entities.description: Physical likenesses between different crop varieties that hinder phenotypic identification

entities.name: GENETIC ADMIXTURES
entities.description: The mixing of different genetic lineages within a variety

entities.name: MOLECULAR MARKER-BASED DNA FINGERPRINTING
entities.description: Techniques using molecular markers to identify genetic profiles for varietal identification

entities.name: VARIETAL IDENTIFICATION
entities.description: The process of distinguishing one crop variety from another

entities.name: MOLECULAR DIAGNOSTICS
entities.description: The use of molecular tools for identification and certification

entities.name: REGULATORY FRAMEWORKS
entities.description: The set of rules and laws governing seed certification and quality control

entities.name: FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: The state of having reliable access to a sufficient quantity of affordable, nutritious food

entities.name: CLIMATE-RESILIENT GROUNDNUT CULTIVARS
entities.description: Groundnut varieties developed to withstand adverse climatic conditions

entities.name: STANDARDIZED PROTOCOLS
entities.description: Uniform procedures for implementing molecular markers on a large scale
